summary: A judge ordered former Baja California governor Ernesto Ruffo and seven others held in Altiplano prison on fiscal huachicol charges, with alleged Defense Ministry links surfacing as the first opposition governor in recent memory is jailed amid US scrutiny of Morena governors' cartel ties. Separately, Mexico filed criminal complaints against ICE agents after 17 Mexicans died in custody, straining ties as US and Mexican teams prepare a third round of USMCA talks covering steel and autos, complicated by Chinese-built trucks operating in Mexico.

lead.h1: Former governor Ruffo's fuel-smuggling hearing drags on with army links alleged
lead.context: The ongoing case against ex-Baja California governor Ruffo includes a businessman accused of running a separate fuel-contraband network involving Defense Ministry personnel, tying the trial to broader concerns about military involvement in huachicol fiscal.
lead.background: Huachicol fiscal is the tax-fraud scheme of smuggling fuel while declaring only part of it, and Ruffo was the first opposition politician to unseat the PRI as Baja California governor.
lead.drivers: The case also implicates businessman Riestra Fernández, accused of running a separate contraband network involving Defense Ministry personnel, while political rhetoric for and against Ruffo's detention keeps building.
lead.implications: The article notes the case matters to both sides: PAN feels bound to defend a historic figure, while Morena, facing its own governors accused of organized-crime ties, gets a chance to shift attention.

items.headline: Mexico files criminal complaints against ICE agents over migrant deaths
items.context: Mexico's government is testing a criminal law route after 17 Mexicans died in ICE custody, raising the diplomatic temperature with Washington over immigration enforcement.
items.background: ICE detention centers are privately run facilities that hold migrants for US federal enforcement operations, a system that has expanded sharply under the current administration.
items.drivers: An Associated Press count puts deaths tied to the enforcement drive at 62, including 17 Mexicans, after agents shot two people and a Venezuelan detainee died of a heart attack during a transfer in Georgia.
items.implications: The complaints raise the diplomatic tone between Mexico and Washington over how migrants are treated in US custody.

items.headline: Court orders pretrial detention for former Baja California governor Ruffo in fuel theft case
items.context: A judge ordered former PAN governor Ernesto Ruffo and seven others held in the Altiplano maximum-security prison after a lengthy initial hearing on charges tied to a fiscal huachicol (fuel theft) scheme, a case that touches both organized crime and high-level political corruption.
items.background: Ernesto Ruffo was Baja California's governor and the first candidate to defeat the PRI in a gubernatorial race, making him a historic figure for the PAN. Huachicol fiscal refers to schemes that evade fuel taxes rather than physically stealing pipeline fuel, and such cases often surface amid Mexico's broader organized-crime and corruption investigations.
items.drivers: After more than a day of hearings, a judge ordered Ruffo and seven co-defendants held for possible organized crime and contraband offenses, sending them to the Altiplano maximum-security prison.
items.implications: The case has become political ammunition for both the ruling party and the opposition, who dispute whether the arrest is a distraction from accusations of ties between Morena and organized crime or a legitimate judicial process.

items.headline: Former Baja California governor Ruffo detained in cartel-linked case
items.context: The arrest of the first opposition governor in recent memory lands amid US scrutiny of Morena governors' alleged cartel ties, reshaping the political fight over organized crime.
items.background: Ernesto Ruffo, Mexico's first opposition governor after defeating the PRI in Baja California in 1989, is now central to a fuel-smuggling probe the government says has run over a year.
items.drivers: Ruffo's arrest gives Morena political relief during one of its most difficult weeks handling the Baja California and Sinaloa cartel-tie scandals.
items.implications: The case reframes the corruption narrative toward opposition figures rather than Morena governors, and marks the first time in Sheinbaum's term an opposition figure has been detained.

items.headline: US and Mexico prepare third round of USMCA review talks
items.context: A US negotiating team will travel to Mexico City to discuss steel, autos and trade as both countries work toward the joint review of the USMCA.
items.background: The USMCA review process now runs annually after the US declined a 16-year extension in July 2026, with negotiators meeting repeatedly to work through outstanding issues before deadlines.
items.drivers: USTR is sending its negotiating team to Mexico City for a third bilateral round from July 21 to 23, covering steel and aluminum, autos, economic security, labor, agriculture and electronic payment services.
items.implications: USTR representative Jamieson Greer credited Economy Secretary Marcelo Ebrard's government with cooperation, suggesting the bilateral track is advancing separately from the broader treaty review.
items.next: The delegations meet July 21 to 23 in Mexico City to advance talks on these sectors.

items.headline: Chinese-made heavy trucks in Mexico complicate bid to ease Trump tariffs
items.context: A growing fleet of China-built trucks operating in Mexico gives Washington another argument to maintain tariff pressure, tying trade relief to scrutiny of Chinese content in Mexico's supply chains.
items.background: USMCA's annual review process now scrutinizes whether Mexico's export platform, especially trucking, is becoming a route for Chinese manufacturers into North America.
items.drivers: Chinese truck import value into Mexico rose more than sevenfold over six years and the number of Chinese truck firms operating there reached about 23, alarming US officials and Mexican manufacturers.
items.implications: The trend complicates Mexican negotiators' push for tariff relief on an integrated export industry, since it undercuts Mexico's case for alignment with the US against China.

items.headline: Grupo Carso buys TotalEnergies' 30% stake in a Gulf of Mexico oil block
items.context: Carlos Slim's conglomerate expands into offshore oil exploration, adding private capital to a sector still dominated by Pemex.
items.background: Grupo Carso is Carlos Slim's conglomerate, which has been expanding into Gulf of Mexico oil and gas blocks as Pemex, facing declining crude output, seeks private partners.
items.drivers: Carso is buying TotalEnergies' 30% stake in Block 30 through a subsidiary, with Harbour Energy retaining 70% and continuing as operator.
items.implications: Slim has said his companies will avoid new joint ventures with Pemex, so the purchase extends his push into oil and gas segments outside state control rather than partnering with the state company.
items.next: The deal's closing is subject to regulatory approval.

items.headline: Banxico deputy governor says inflation risks remain tilted to the upside
items.context: Jonathan Heath said the balance of risks has not turned favorable despite June's slowdown, a signal the central bank may hold off on further rate cuts.
items.background: Banxico targets 3% inflation, give or take one percentage point, and Jonathan Heath sits on its governing board that sets the policy rate.
items.drivers: Heath noted June's inflation reading, at 3.37% annually, understated how favorable the second half of the month actually was, but he questioned whether the improvement will last through the rest of the year.
items.implications: His comment that the balance of risks is not yet favorable suggests Banxico may proceed cautiously on further rate moves.
items.next: Heath said the balance of risks is not yet favorable, signaling continued close monitoring of incoming data before any shift in stance.

standing.text: The peso trades at 17.52 pesos to the dollar; inflation is 3.37%; the policy rate is 6.50%.

live.tmpl: the peso trades at {v} pesos to the dollar
